code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of Triples about an astronaut (or related entities) into a
    fluent, natural‑language description.  The implementation uses richer
    templates, handles direction‑reversed predicates (e.g. backupPilot where the
    object is the person and the subject is the mission), and builds a short
    paragraph by joining clauses with periods instead of a single comma‑list.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Helper to strip surrounding quotes from literal strings.
    def clean(val: str) -> str:
        return val.strip('\"')

    # Templates for predicates where the subject is the astronaut.
    astronaut_templates = {
        "almaMater": lambda s, o: f"{s} studied at {clean(o)}",
        "birthDate": lambda s, o: f"{s} was born on {clean(o)}",
        "birthName": lambda s, o: f"{s} was originally named {clean(o)}",
        "birthPlace": lambda s, o: f"{s} was born in {clean(o)}",
        "dateOfRetirement": lambda s, o: f"{s} retired in {clean(o)}",
        "occupation": lambda s, o: f"{s} was a {clean(o)}",
        "status": lambda s, o: f"{s} is {clean(o).lower()}",
        "timeInSpace": lambda s, o: f"{s} spent {clean(o)} minutes in space",
        "mission": lambda s, o: f"{s} was a crew member of {clean(o)}",
        "selectedByNasa": lambda s, o: f"{s} was selected by NASA in {clean(o)}",
        "deathDate": lambda s, o: f"{s} died on {clean(o)}",
        "nationality": lambda s, o: f"{s} was a {clean(o)} citizen",
        "servedAsChiefOfTheAstronautOfficeIn": lambda s, o: f"{s} served as chief of the Astronaut Office in {clean(o)}",
        "title": lambda s, o: f"{s} held the title \"{clean(o)}\"",
        "ribbonAward": lambda s, o: f"{s} received the {clean(o)} ribbon",
        "award": lambda s, o: f"{s} received the award {clean(o)}",
        "awards": lambda s, o: f"{s} earned {clean(o)} awards",
        "deathPlace": lambda s, o: f"{s} died in {clean(o)}",
    }

    # Templates for predicates where the subject is a mission (or other entity)
    # and the object is a person or organization.
    mission_templates = {
        "operator": lambda s, o: f"{clean(s)} was operated by {clean(o)}",
        "backupPilot": lambda s, o: f"{clean(o)} was a backup pilot for {clean(s)}",
        "commander": lambda s, o: f"{clean(o)} was the commander of {clean(s)}",
        "crewMembers": lambda s, o: f"{clean(o)} was a crew member of {clean(s)}",
        "representative": lambda s, o: f"{clean(o)} was the representative of {clean(s)}",
    }

    # Build clauses.
    clauses = []
    for triple in triples:
        if triple.predicate in astronaut_templates:
            clause = astronaut_templates[triple.predicate](triple.subject, triple.object)
        elif triple.predicate in mission_templates:
            clause = mission_templates[triple.predicate](triple.subject, triple.object)
        else:
            # Fallback generic handling.
            clause = f"{triple.subject} {triple.predicate} {triple.object}"
        clauses.append(clause)

    # Remove duplicate subject mentions when they appear consecutively.
    merged = []
    prev_subj = None
    for clause in clauses:
        subj = clause.split()[0]
        if subj == prev_subj:
            # drop the repeated subject, keep the rest of the clause
            merged.append(clause[len(subj) + 1 :])
        else:
            merged.append(clause)
            prev_subj = subj

    # Join clauses into a short paragraph.
    sentence = ". ".join(merged) + "."
    # Capitalise first character
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END

```python
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of Triples about an astronaut (or related entities) into a
    single, readable sentence.  The function uses a small set of handcrafted
    templates for the supported predicates and joins the resulting clauses
    with commas and a final “and”.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Basic templates for the most common astronaut predicates.
    # Each template receives (subject, object) and returns a clause string.
    templates = {
        "almaMater": lambda s, o: f"studied at {o}",
        "birthDate": lambda s, o: f"was born on {o}",
        "birthName": lambda s, o: f"was originally named {o}",
        "birthPlace": lambda s, o: f"was born in {o}",
        "dateOfRetirement": lambda s, o: f"retired in {o}",
        "occupation": lambda s, o: f"worked as a {o}",
        "status": lambda s, o: f"had the status {o}",
        "timeInSpace": lambda s, o: f"spent {o} minutes in space",
        "mission": lambda s, o: f"flew on {o}",
        "selectedByNasa": lambda s, o: f"was selected by NASA in {o}",
        "deathDate": lambda s, o: f"died on {o}",
        "nationality": lambda s, o: f"was a {o} citizen",
        "servedAsChiefOfTheAstronautOfficeIn": lambda s, o: f"served as chief of the Astronaut Office in {o}",
        "title": lambda s, o: f"held the title \"{o}\"",
        "ribbonAward": lambda s, o: f"received the {o} ribbon",
        "operator": lambda s, o: f"was operated by {o}",
        "backupPilot": lambda s, o: f"had {o} as backup pilot",
        "commander": lambda s, o: f"was commanded by {o}",
        "crewMembers": lambda s, o: f"had crew member {o}",
        "representative": lambda s, o: f"was represented by {o}",
        "alternativeName": lambda s, o: f"was also known as {o}",
        "awards": lambda s, o: f"earned {o} awards",
        "award": lambda s, o: f"received the award {o}",
        "deathPlace": lambda s, o: f"died in {o}",
        # Generic fallback
        "default": lambda s, o: f"{s} {s} {o}",
    }

    # Build clauses; keep subject for first clause to allow pronoun later.
    clauses = []
    primary_subject = triples[0].subject
    for i, triple in enumerate(triples):
        tmpl = templates.get(triple.predicate, templates["default"])
        clause = tmpl(triple.subject, triple.object)
        # For the first clause, prepend the subject if the template does not already include it.
        if i == 0 and not clause.lower().startswith(primary_subject.lower()):
            clause = f"{primary_subject} {clause}"
        clauses.append(clause)

    # Join clauses into a fluent sentence.
    if len(clauses) == 1:
        sentence = clauses[0] + "."
    else:
        sentence = ", ".join(clauses[:-1]) + ", and " + clauses[-1] + "."
    # Capitalise first character
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
```
